scenarios.opening_query: A gym I quit two years ago is still charging my card and support just loops me in circles.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > General || Consumer Law > Banking
scenarios.hidden_fact: My bank is the deeper problem — I've submitted four written stop-payment and dispute requests and they keep honoring the charges anyway, against their own regulations.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The bank's the real problem — four written stop-payment disputes and they keep honoring the charges anyway.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the bank's dispute-handling failure is the operative claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The bank blocks each charge when I ask — it's the gym generating fresh 'contracts' I never signed that has to be stopped.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The bank blocks each one — it's the gym inventing fresh 'contracts' that has to stop.
scenarios.vetting_note: Merchant-vs-bank defendant selection again in a subscription-trap setting.

scenarios.opening_query: The cremation company handled my mother's remains so carelessly that our family is traumatized.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > General || General Litigation > General Torts/Privacy
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's beyond bad service — they gave us the wrong person's ashes and can't locate Mom's; the claim is the mishandling-of-remains tort with real emotional-distress damages.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They gave us the wrong person's ashes and can't find Mom's — that's the mishandling-of-remains tort, not a service complaint.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether remains were actually lost/switched creating a dignitary tort
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The remains are right — it's the billing: they charged triple the signed contract and threaten collections on the difference.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The remains are right — they charged triple the contract and threaten collections.
scenarios.vetting_note: Funeral-services intakes split exactly on this harm-type fact.

scenarios.opening_query: An online course guru took thousands from me for a 'mentorship' that never materialized.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > General || Consumer Law > Problems Between Consumers
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's not a company — one individual selling access person-to-person over payment apps, no business entity anywhere.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's just an individual — person-to-person payment apps, no business entity anywhere.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the seller is a business or private individual
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a registered LLC with employees and a refund policy they simply ignore.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a registered LLC with staff and a refund policy they ignore.
scenarios.vetting_note: Guru-economy version of the business/individual boundary.

scenarios.opening_query: My storage facility 'lost' my unit's contents while I was deployed overseas.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Storage Units || Administrative Law > Military/Veterans || Consumer Law > Storage Units
scenarios.hidden_fact: They auctioned it during my deployment despite my SCRA notice on file — servicemember protections prohibit exactly that, and JAG says it's an SCRA enforcement case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They auctioned it during deployment despite my SCRA notice on file — JAG says that's an SCRA enforcement case.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I'm a civilian contractor, not military — the case is their lien-sale notice failures under the storage statutes.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I'm a civilian contractor — it's their lien-sale notice failures under the storage laws.
scenarios.vetting_note: SCRA status transforms the storage-lien case; deployment context invites the question.

scenarios.opening_query: A travel company canceled our once-in-a-lifetime trip and is offering vouchers instead of the refund.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > General || General Litigation > General Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: There are 200+ of us in a group who all lost the same trip — we're organizing, and the question is a class action, not individual refunds.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: There are over 200 of us from the same trip organizing together — it's really a class-action question.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether collective/class litigation is the posture
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's just our family's $8,000 — and their contract's voucher clause looks unenforceable for a company-initiated cancellation.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Just our $8,000 — and their voucher clause looks unenforceable when THEY cancel.
scenarios.vetting_note: Scale disclosure moves individual consumer claim to collective litigation.

scenarios.opening_query: My identity was stolen last year and I thought it was handled, but new problems keep surfacing.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Identity Theft || Criminal Law > Expunction
scenarios.hidden_fact: The newest problem: the thief gave MY name at an arrest, so there's a criminal record under my identity — I need the court record corrected and cleared, which is its own judicial process.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The thief gave my name when arrested — there's a criminal record under my identity now, and clearing that is its own court process.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a criminal-record identity mix-up needs judicial correction
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's financial only — two new accounts surfaced on my credit report this month and the furnishers won't remove them.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Financial only — two new fraudulent accounts on my report the furnishers won't remove.
scenarios.vetting_note: Criminal-identity-theft aftermath requires record-clearing practice, not FCRA work.
